summary: The better-information-ui OpenSpec change is well-formed, complete, and ready for implementation. All required artifacts (proposal, design, tasks, spec delta) are present and pass automated validation. The scope is tightly bounded to the NiceGUI operator surfaces in scripts/web_app_hub.py, the information model is concretely defined, and the 13-task breakdown gives an implementation steward a clear ordered sequence to follow. The spec deltas correctly include both MODIFIED (Operator Overview, Round Detail Timeline) and ADDED (Modern NiceGUI Information Layout) requirements sections with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN scenarios. No protocol violations, missing files, or structural issues were found.

findings.detail: Automated validation (scripts/validate-openspec-change.py) returned ok=true with zero errors and zero warnings. All required files present with top-level headings. tasks.md contains checkbox tasks. spec delta includes Requirement/Scenario structure and MODIFIED/ADDED sections.

Scope is unambiguous: operator overview and selected-round timeline surfaces in scripts/web_app_hub.py only. Explicit out-of-scope list covers auth, non-operator views, write operations, legacy raw HTML/JS redesign, Hub/MCP/Kubernetes contract changes. Backward-compatible JSON field additions are explicitly permitted.

Normalized timeline entry fields are named and described in design.md (entry_id, sequence, timestamp, agent, action, handoff, reason_for_handoff, status, source, detail). This gives implementation a concrete target data shape without over-prescribing the source adapter internals.

The change explicitly defines the deduplication boundary: only exact replay duplicates with the same stable source identity may be suppressed. Same-agent or same-agent-pair repeated exchanges with distinct ids, timestamps, or sequence positions must remain visible. This is a non-obvious constraint and it is well-specified in both design.md and the spec delta scenario.

Component direction in design.md names specific NiceGUI primitives (ui.table, ui.tabs, ui.tab_panels, ui.expansion, chips, badges, tooltips, splitters, drawers, dialogs). Custom CSS is scoped to behavior NiceGUI components do not provide. This is actionable for an implementation steward.

Design and spec both specify desktop (dense scanning, no horizontal overflow, scoped long-value containers) and mobile (stacking, wrapping, secondary diagnostics collapse) behavior. Long content examples are listed (round ids, agent names, handoff targets, branch refs, validation summaries).

Tasks 1.1-1.2 (inventory and field definition) precede implementation tasks 1.3-1.8, followed by verification 1.9-1.10 and testing 1.11-1.13. The sequence is logical. Tasks 1.1 and 1.2 are pre-implementation discovery steps that the design partially satisfies conceptually, but leaving them open is correct since implementation must verify against actual source code.

author.md notes that clarifier.md and explorer.md were not present in the session findings. These appear to be optional or upstream-session artifacts. The four core change artifacts are present and complete; no action required.

Design specifies no-spend checks for normalization, duplicate preservation, concise defaults, endpoint compatibility, and responsive layout. Task 1.11 operationalizes this as focused tests or fixtures. This gives implementation a testable acceptance bar without requiring expensive end-to-end infrastructure.
